signals.title: USD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Dis
signals.text: JPY 1W avg +1.081% (n=7) outperforms USD 1W avg -0.872% (n=7) with a G8 spread of 1.953%; the 290bp US-JGB yield gap creates USD/JPY upside pressure. Crowded JPY short -88.5k sets up squeeze risk for JPY, reinforcing USD/JPY upside. The next BoJ meeting may resolve the yield disadvantage and squeeze risk.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike Bias
signals.text: AUD COT LF long +48.3k (74% long) creates crowded-long unwind risk — AUD/USD closed 0.7208 Friday; RBA at 4.10% with hike bias (0% priced) sustains the bid but any disappointment triggers the unwind. The RBA meeting on 5 May determines direction in resolving this setup. Carry of +35bp for AUD vs USD may also support the pair.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Trump Tariff Escalation
signals.text: EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723; Trump tariff escalation on EU autos sets up downside pressure toward 1.16 — EUR COT short -18.3k reinforces the bias. The next ECB meeting is likely to influence the pair, but the primary driver is the tariff escalation. The carry disadvantage of -175bp for EUR vs USD also weighs on the pair.

signals.title: GBP/USD — BoE Hold
signals.text: GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3575; BoE at 3.75% with Hold bias is likely to sustain the pair, but the strong long positioning of +32.6k (67.3% long) creates unwind risk. The next BoE meeting determines direction in resolving this setup. Carry of 0bp for GBP vs USD is neutral.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Oil Price Impact
signals.text: USD/CAD closed Friday at 1.3588; WTI crude oil price drop is likely to pressure USD/CAD, but the CAD COT short -54.7k (23.8% long) creates a potential squeeze. The next BoC meeting is likely to influence the pair, and the carry disadvantage of -150bp for CAD vs USD also affects the pair.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.5898, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but specs short −16.8k sets up a notable divergence with the RBNZ's hike bias, where 40% of a hike is priced. This creates a short-squeeze risk as the market is positioned against the direction the RBNZ is signalling. The unwind of these positions is likely to be catalyzed by the RBNZ meeting on 27 May.
